stage.description: AMM x×y=k, impermanent loss formula, Aave health factors, yield strategies, flash loans, and MEV.

elements.content: How AMMs Work
The math behind every decentralized swap

$1B+
Daily DEX vol
x × y = k

x = Token A reserve · y = Token B reserve · k = constant

Buy Token B → x increases, y decreases → Token B price rises
The curve never touches zero — infinite slippage before reserves empty
Uniswap V2 uses this formula. V3 concentrates liquidity in price ranges for 4,000× better capital efficiency.

elements.content: Impermanent Loss
Why providing liquidity is not free money
IL = 2√P / (1+P) − 1

P = price ratio change between the two tokens

2× price divergence = 5.7% IL · 5× divergence = 25.5% IL
IL is only 'impermanent' if prices return to entry ratio — otherwise it becomes permanent loss
IL only applies to AMM LPs. Order book LPs and single-sided vaults have different risk profiles.

elements.content: Lending & Liquidations
How collateralized borrowing works — and how it fails

Aave: Lending Protocol

Supply collateral → borrow up to LTV limit

Health Factor = Collateral × Liq. Threshold ÷ Debt

Health Factor < 1.0 → liquidation

Liquidators repay your debt and receive your collateral at a 5–15% discount. They race to liquidate first.

Liquidation Cascade

ETH drops 30% in an hour:
1. Thousands of health factors hit <1.0
2. Liquidation bots sell collateral on DEXes
3. Selling pushes ETH lower
4. More positions become undercollateralized
5. Cascade accelerates

May 2021: $600M liquidated in hours.

elements.content: Yield & Risk
Every percentage point of yield has a corresponding risk

Yield Strategies

Simple: Supply to Aave → earn supply APY
Medium: LP on Uniswap → earn trading fees + rewards
Advanced: Leveraged yield farming → borrow to amplify yield

Risk ladder: as yield increases, so does smart contract risk, liquidation risk, and IL.

Smart Contract Risk

Every DeFi protocol is code — code has bugs:
• Reentrancy: The DAO ($60M, 2016)
• Oracle manipulation: Mango Markets ($114M, 2022)
• Logic error: Beanstalk ($182M, 2022)

Audit ≠ safe. Multiple audits help. Bug bounties help more. Never risk more than you can lose.

elements.content: DeFi Ecosystem
Key primitives beyond basic swaps and lending

DEX Aggregators

1inch, Paraswap — split orders across multiple DEXes for best price. Saves 0.5–5% on large trades.

Liquid Staking

Stake ETH → receive stETH (liquid). Earn 3–4% APY while keeping liquidity for DeFi. Lido: $20B+ TVL.

Real Yield

Protocols sharing actual revenue with token holders. GMX: trading fees → GLP stakers. Sustainable vs inflationary yield.

elements.content: DeFi: Opportunity & Risk
The most powerful financial infrastructure — with no safety net

DeFi vs TradFi

DeFi advantages:
• 24/7 operation, no closing times
• Non-custodial — you keep your keys
• Permissionless — no KYC required
• Composable — protocols stack like Lego
• Transparent — all code is public

DeFi risks:

• Smart contract bugs (no insurance)
• No chargebacks — mistakes are permanent
• Regulatory uncertainty in most countries
• Gas fees can eat small positions
• UI/UX still early — easy to make costly errors

Never invest more than you understand.

elements.content: 🏦 Lending Protocol Architecture — Aave & Compound
How algorithmic interest rates work in practice

Aave and Compound use utilization rate to set interest rates algorithmically. Utilization = borrowed / total deposited. When utilization is low, rates are low (incentivize borrowing). As it approaches 100%, rates spike sharply to incentivize repayment and new deposits.


Borrowers must maintain a health factor above 1.0. If collateral value drops below the liquidation threshold, anyone can liquidate the position — taking the collateral at a discount. This keeps the protocol solvent.

📊 Utilization Rate
Borrowed ÷ Total Deposited

📈 Rate Curve
High utilization → high APR

⚖️ Health Factor
Must stay above 1.0 or liquidation

🤖 Liquidation
Bots monitor and liquidate instantly

As it approaches 100%, rates spike sharply to incentivize repayment and new deposits. Borrowers must maintain a health factor above 1.0. If collateral value drops below the liquidation threshold, anyone can liquidate the position — taking the collateral at a discount. This keeps the protocol solvent

elements.content: ⚙️ Stablecoin Mechanisms
The three models for maintaining a dollar peg

Fiat-backed (USDT, USDC) — reserves of real dollars back each token 1:1. Centralized, trusted counterparty risk.


Crypto-backed (DAI) — over-collateralized with ETH/WBTC. Trustless but capital inefficient (need $150 locked to mint $100 of DAI).


Algorithmic (UST/LUNA) — code controls supply via mint/burn with a paired token. Capital efficient but fragile — a death spiral wiped $40B in 72 hours in May 2022.

💵 Fiat-Backed
1:1 USD reserve — centralized

🔒 Crypto-Backed
150%+ collateral — trustless

⚙️ Algorithmic
Code-managed supply — high risk

💀 Death Spiral
Algo failed: $40B lost in 3 days

questions.question: In a constant-product AMM (x × y = k), what happens to the price of token Y when you buy a large amount of Y?

options.label: Price of Y decreases
Price of Y increases
Price stays constant
k increases

questions.analysis: Buying Y reduces Y's pool reserve, so the price rises. The constant k never changes — only the ratio between x and y moves.

questions.question: What is impermanent loss?

options.label: The gas fee paid when depositing into a liquidity pool
The difference between holding tokens vs providing liquidity when prices diverge
The interest paid to borrowers on Aave
A penalty for removing liquidity before 30 days

questions.analysis: Impermanent loss = the value you'd have by just holding vs the value you actually have in the pool after a price move. It becomes permanent if you withdraw.

questions.question: On Aave, if your health factor drops below 1.0, what happens?

options.label: Your interest rate doubles
Your position is liquidated
You receive a margin call email
Borrowing is paused for 24 hours

questions.analysis: Health factor < 1.0 means your collateral is worth less than your debt at the liquidation threshold. Liquidators can seize your collateral.

questions.question: Which TWO statements are true about flash loans?

options.label: They must be borrowed and repaid within a single transaction
They require collateral worth 150% of the loan
If not repaid, the entire transaction reverts
They are only available to KYC-verified users

questions.analysis: Flash loans are uncollateralized and atomic — borrow and repay in one tx, or the whole tx fails. Anyone can use them.

questions.question: Explain how a DeFi liquidation cascade can amplify a market crash. Which sequence correctly describes how this happens?

options.label: ETH drops → health factors fall below 1.0 → bots liquidate collateral on DEXes → selling pressure pushes ETH lower → more positions become undercollateralized → further liquidations
ETH drops → Aave pauses lending → users manually sell collateral → price stabilizes → no further liquidations occur
ETH drops → liquidators buy ETH at a premium → price recovers → health factors return above 1.0 → cascade stops
ETH drops → smart contracts freeze withdrawals → users are protected from liquidation until the price recovers

questions.analysis: When ETH drops sharply, thousands of health factors simultaneously hit the liquidation threshold. Liquidation bots sell the collateral on DEXes to repay debt, which adds selling pressure and pushes ETH lower still. This makes more positions undercollateralized, triggering further liquidations — a self-reinforcing cascade.

questions.question: What is the x × y = k formula and what does it guarantee?

options.label: It sets the interest rate based on utilization
It ensures the product of two token reserves stays constant, automatically adjusting price
It calculates impermanent loss for LP positions
It determines the liquidation price for a collateralized position

questions.analysis: In a Uniswap-style AMM with reserves x and y, x × y must equal k (a constant). When you buy token y (reducing y), x must increase to keep k constant. This automatically raises the price of y. No order book needed — the math prices the market.

questions.question: What caused the UST/LUNA collapse in May 2022?

options.label: A smart contract bug in the Terra protocol
A death spiral where UST de-peg triggered LUNA hyperinflation which further broke the peg
An oracle manipulation attack on the price feed
A governance vote to pause all withdrawals

questions.analysis: UST maintained its peg by allowing 1 UST to be burned to mint $1 of LUNA. When a large seller broke the peg, people rushed to burn UST for LUNA and sell — hyperinflating LUNA supply, destroying its value, making the UST backing worthless. $40B evaporated in 72 hours.

questions.question: What is MEV (Maximal Extractable Value)?

options.label: The maximum APY achievable through yield farming
The maximum amount a validator can earn from block rewards
Profit extracted by reordering or inserting transactions within a block
The fee limit set by the protocol for flash loans

questions.analysis: MEV is extracted by validators or bots who can see pending transactions and manipulate ordering. Sandwich attacks front-run large swaps. Arbitrage bots exploit price differences across pools. Liquidation bots race to liquidate underwater positions. Estimated $1B+ extracted annually on Ethereum.
